evals.name: multi-file-feature-runs-phased-loop
evals.prompt: I want to add a 'saved searches' feature to our app: users can name and persist a search query, see a list of their saved searches, and re-run one with a click. It'll touch the API, the data layer, and the frontend. Help me build it.
evals.expected_output: The agent recognizes this as a non-trivial, multi-file feature and runs the phased loop. It opens a visible per-phase task tracker, dispatches 2-3 parallel read-only explorer subagents to understand existing search/persistence/UI patterns, then reads the files they surface. CRITICALLY, it asks structured multiple-choice clarifying questions (scope, storage, edge cases) BEFORE writing any implementation code, and waits for answers. It does not jump straight to coding.

assertions.description: Uses structured multiple-choice questions, not prose
Reads the files the explorers surface
assertions.description_long: The agent states it will read the key files identified by explorer subagents before designing.

assertions.description: Does not begin implementation before clarification/architecture
assertions.description_long: No implementation code is written before the clarify and architecture phases complete with user input.

evals.name: typo-hotfix-declines-heavy-loop
evals.prompt: There's a typo in the login page heading: it says 'Welome' instead of 'Welcome'. Fix it.
evals.expected_output: The agent recognizes this is a trivial single-line/typo change that falls under 'when NOT to fire'. It does NOT open a seven-phase task tracker, does NOT dispatch explorer/architect/reviewer subagents, and does NOT run a batch of clarifying questions. It just locates the string and fixes it directly, optionally noting that the heavy feature-development loop is unnecessary overhead here.

assertions.description: Declines the multi-phase loop for a trivial change
Does NOT dispatch parallel exploration/architecture subagents
Does NOT spin up the full seven-phase tracker

assertions.description_long: The agent fixes the typo directly rather than running discovery/exploration/clarify phases.

evals.name: design-system-presents-multiple-options
evals.prompt: Design the notifications system for our product: in-app, email, and eventually push. I want to see the options before we commit to anything.
evals.expected_output: The agent treats this as a scope/design request and runs the loop with emphasis on exploration and architecture. After exploring and asking structured clarifying questions, it dispatches 2-3 parallel architect subagents with DIFFERENT framings (minimal-change / clean / pragmatic), then presents the distinct approaches with trade-offs and a recommendation, and asks the user to choose one via a structured question before any implementation. It does not pick an architecture unilaterally.

assertions.description: Asks the user to choose an approach
Still runs clarifying questions before designing
Does not commit to one architecture without the user
assertions.description_long: The agent presents options and waits for the user to select rather than picking and implementing on its own.

evals.name: scrubbing-guard-no-private-references
evals.prompt: Walk me through how this feature-development workflow handles a multi-file feature, end to end.
evals.expected_output: A description of the seven-phase loop using only generic, portable terminology. No references to any private project, internal codename, project-specific slash command, or hardcoded sub-agent name appear.

assertions.description: No private project names, codenames, or project-specific command/paths leak
